2603.22785.title: Exposure-Normalized Bed and Chair Fall Rates via Continuous AI Monitoring
2603.22785.paper_summary: This retrospective cohort study used continuous AI monitoring to estimate fall rates by exposure time rather than occupied bed-days. From August 2024 to December 2025, 3,980 eligible monitoring units contributed 292,914 hourly rows, yielding probability-weighted rates of 17.8 falls per 1,000 chair exposure-hours and 4.3 per 1,000 bed exposure-hours. Within the study window, 43 adjudicated falls matched the monitoring pipeline, and 40 linked to eligible exposure hours for the primary Poisson model, producing an adjusted chair-versus-bed rate ratio of 2.35 (95% confidence interval 0.87 to 6.33; p=0.0907). In a separate broader observation cohort (n=32 deduplicated events), 6 of 7 direct chair falls involved footrest-positioning failures. Because this was an observational study in a single health system, these findings remain hypothesis-generating and support testing safer chair setups rather than using chairs less.
2603.22785.paper_summary_zh: 這項回顧性隊列研究使用持續的人工智慧監測來根據暴露時間估算跌倒率，而不是根據佔用床日。從2024年8月到2025年12月，3,980個符合條件的監測單位貢獻了292,914小時的數據行，產生了每1,000小時椅子暴露17.8次跌倒和每1,000小時床鋪暴露4.3次的概率加權率。在研究期間，43起裁定的跌倒事件與監測管道相符，並且40起與主要泊松模型的符合條件的暴露小時相關，產生了調整後的椅子對床的比率為2.35（95%置信區間0.87至6.33；p=0.0907）。在一個單獨的更廣泛觀察隊列中（n=32去重事件），7起直接椅子跌倒中有6起涉及腳踏板定位失敗。由於這是一項在單一健康系統中的觀察性研究，這些發現仍然是生成假設的，並支持測試更安全的椅子設置，而不是減少使用椅子。
2603.22785.author: Paolo Gabriel et.al.
2603.22785.authors: Paolo Gabriel, Peter Rehani, Zack Drumm, Tyler Troy, Tiffany Wyatt, Narinder Singh
